description: Unicode (NFC) handling. The title contains a precomposed character (U+00E9, é) and an em dash (U+2014). JCS requires NFC; implementations decomposing to NFD before serializing will produce different bytes and a different hash. Implementations MUST reproduce the canonical form and SHA-256 byte-exactly.

vectors.name: Unicode (precomposed) body canonicalization and SHA-256
vectors.description: Producer-supplied body fields with title 'café — test'. The é is U+00E9 (precomposed); the em dash is U+2014. Apply JCS, then SHA-256, hex-encode lowercase.
implementer_notes: If your implementation decomposes the title to NFD before serialization, the bytes for é become U+0065 U+0301 (two code points) and you'll get a different hash. JCS requires NFC.

If your serializer escapes non-ASCII characters as \uXXXX (e.g. Python's `json.dumps(..., ensure_ascii=True)`), you'll also get a different hash. Use ensure_ascii=False (Python) or the equivalent in your language.
